_comment: F1-S9 slice 2 (issue #12): the mutation-testing baseline for snowflake/assert_dev_ci_grants.py + snowflake/check_forbidden_grants.py + snowflake/validate_migrations.py (the last added in the ready round -- a Codex finding: check_forbidden_grants.py directly calls validate_migrations.find_candidate_migration_files to decide which files get scanned for a forbidden PUBLIC grant, so it's genuinely part of the grant-boundary security surface, not unrelated filename/render validation). Re-measured after two later Codex findings on this same PR: (1) every python invocation in the mutation-testing and snowflake-migrations CI jobs now runs under `-P` (PYTHONSAFEPATH) to close a module-shadowing class -- see ci.yml's own comments; (2) check_forbidden_grants.py's own `from validate_migrations import ...` had to become a path-anchored importlib load (`_load_sibling_module`) since -P removes the automatic sys.path entry that bare import relied on, adding ~16 mutants to check_forbidden_grants.py's own surface (its `main()`'s pre-existing survivors are unchanged; the two NEW survivors are both inside a `# pragma: no cover`-marked, provably-unreachable defensive branch in _load_sibling_module -- equivalent mutants, not a real gap; see the function's own docstring). Earlier in this same PR's history, the bounded survivor-triage skim killed 2 real survivors (both continue -> break mutants in find_unexpected_user_role_grants, on the empty-role-field and PUBLIC-role branches respectively -- a genuine row-ordering-dependent gap where an empty-role or PUBLIC row sorting before a violating row would have silently stopped the scan; see test_assert_dev_ci_grants.py's own new tests). Re-measured on main via `mutmut run` + `mutmut export-cicd-stats` (19 Jul 2026, mutmut==3.6.0) -- deterministic across repeated runs at this final state (a rare, pre-existing 1-mutant flake was observed once during an earlier wider-scope re-measurement, always in the score-IMPROVING direction, noted in the PR body -- not present across the runs that produced this final number). check_mutation_score.py fails the check if a later run's killed/(killed+survived) ratio drops below this baseline's ratio, if total drops below this baseline's total, or if any UNRESOLVED_MUTANT_CATEGORIES count (no_tests/suspicious/timeout/segfault) rises above this baseline's (all zero here). Recorded as raw counts (not a pre-computed percentage) so a reviewer can recompute the ratio by hand and so a future re-baseline (after genuinely strengthening the test suite, or a legitimate scope change like this one) is an honest, auditable count update, not an opaque number. Per the operator scoping decision on issue #12: this starts at whatever the surface's REAL baseline is, not an aspirational target -- 593 mutants, 437 killed, 156 survived (~73.69% mutation score) on this final pass. The four UNRESOLVED_MUTANT_CATEGORIES keys below are now RECORDED EXPLICITLY (all 0), not left to load_baseline's implicit default (independent factory-security-reviewer finding, F1-S9 slice 2, issue #12): load_baseline now fail-closes on any REQUIRED_STATS_KEYS field missing from this file, the same schema load_stats already enforces on the current run's JSON -- a rebaseline that silently OMITTED a field (e.g. 'total') would otherwise default to 0 and disable that field's regression check entirely.
